message:
chore(beads): close bd-l0ykk with verified fuzz_env evidence

Verified crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_env.rs covers the
full env-var surface (getenv, secure_getenv, setenv, unsetenv, putenv,
clearenv) with a BTreeMap shadow oracle, EINVAL assertion for
malformed names, and leaked-arena putenv ownership. 1356 corpus seeds
committed. Build verified via rch with CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_fuzz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -587,7 +587,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-am9u.3","title":"Increase CPU affinity mask from 128 bytes (1024 CPUs) to support larger systems","description":"FILE: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:1804
CURRENT: let mut mask = [0u8; 128] — supports max 1024 CPUs.
PROBLEM: Large servers can have >1024 logical CPUs (e.g., AMD EPYC with 256 cores + SMT = 512, multi-socket = 1024+). The mask should be sized to the actual system or use a reasonable upper bound.
FIX: Either use a larger static buffer (e.g., 512 bytes = 4096 CPUs) or dynamically query the kernel for the actual affinity mask size. The sched_getaffinity syscall returns the actual size used, so we could do a two-pass approach.
IMPACT: Low — only affects very large servers. Current 1024 CPU limit is sufficient for most systems.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:53.388076511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:14:19.511676710Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:14:19.510452740Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-am9u.3","depends_on_id":"bd-am9u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:53.388076511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-am9u.4","title":"Make default thread stack size configurable via environment variable","description":"FILES: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:2488,2494
CURRENT: ATTR_DEFAULT_STACK_SIZE = 2 * 1024 * 1024 (2 MiB), ATTR_MIN_STACK_SIZE = 16384 (16 KiB)
CONTEXT: glibc uses a default of 8 MiB, musl uses 128 KiB. Our 2 MiB is a reasonable middle ground but differs from both.
FIX: Keep 2 MiB as default but allow override via FRANKENLIBC_THREAD_STACK_SIZE env var. The minimum should match PTHREAD_STACK_MIN from the system.
IMPACT: Low — 2 MiB works for most applications. Some embedded or memory-constrained scenarios might want smaller stacks.
CONSIDERATION: Don't read env var on every thread creation — cache it at init time.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:54.503610731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:15:51.871640506Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:15:51.870681077Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-am9u.4","depends_on_id":"bd-am9u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:54.503610731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-am9u.5","title":"Fix _SC_TTY_NAME_MAX from 32 to match system reality","description":"FILE: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:1924
CURRENT: _SC_TTY_NAME_MAX = 32
PROBLEM: While glibc returns 32 for this, some systems have longer TTY names (especially for pseudo-terminals with long paths like /dev/pts/XXX). The constant should match glibc's behavior for compatibility.
FIX: Keep 32 to match glibc behavior but add a comment documenting the rationale. This is actually correct for glibc compat.
IMPACT: Very low — TTY names are almost always < 32 chars.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:05.574339644Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:16:15.748586062Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:16:14.432724344Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-am9u.5","depends_on_id":"bd-am9u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:05.574339644Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1339,"issue_id":"bd-am9u.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Verified: _SC_TTY_NAME_MAX=32 matches glibc behavior. No change needed.","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:16:15Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-anoe7","title":"fuzz: pathname encoding edge cases (NUL, UTF-8, long, .. traversal, symlinks)","description":"## Scope
Add crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_pathname.rs targeting the pathname-parsing + canonicalization surface: realpath, canonicalize_file_name, basename/dirname, access/faccessat, stat/lstat/fstatat, readlink/readlinkat. These share a common pathname-bytes input channel and are the classic CVE surface (CVE-2018-1000001 realpath OOB, CVE-2016-1234 glibc glob).

## Archetype
1 + 3: Crash-detector on the impl + **differential** against libc for path operations that don't touch fs state (basename/dirname) and for the few that do (stat/readlink) over a pre-seeded tmpdir with a known symlink graph.

## Inputs
struct PathnameFuzzInput {
  op: u8,                 // 0=realpath, 1=basename, 2=dirname, 3=stat, 4=lstat, 5=readlink, 6=access
  bytes: Vec<u8>,         // arbitrary — rejected if it contains interior NUL (CString won't build)
  buf_size: u16,          // for readlink
  flags: i32,             // for faccessat etc.
}

## Invariants
- Never panic
- For basename/dirname: result string length <= original + some constant; bytes are a suffix/prefix of the input
- For realpath: if non-null, resolved path has no '//' or '/./' or trailing '/', all components are canonical
- For stat family: non-negative rc implies st_dev/st_ino written; errno on failure is in the documented set
- Host parity: our errno family matches libc for inert paths

## Seed corpus
- empty, '/', '.', '..', '/tmp', '/proc/self', '/dev/null', '/etc/passwd', '/////' (collapsing slashes), '../../../..', path with unicode (é, 中, emoji), path with high-bit bytes, path at NAME_MAX (255), path at PATH_MAX (4096)

## Expected bug classes
- PATH_MAX overflow, NAME_MAX overflow, NUL-embedded path, UTF-8 boundary split, symlink loop detection, '..' collapsing vs preserving, trailing-slash semantics, basename('') = '', dirname('/') = '/'

## Score
5 × 4 × /3 ≈ 6.7 — high (path CVEs are a historical goldmine).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cc-libc","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:39:57.580798569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:12:00.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-anoe7","depends_on_id":"bd-jy4qu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:01:45.216777141Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"closed_at":"2026-04-23T23:12:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"Done: harness at crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_pathname.rs covers all bead-required ops (realpath, canonicalize_file_name, basename, dirname, stat_family, access, readlink, at_family=faccessat/fstatat/readlinkat), 263-entry seed corpus, 2.8M runs clean at 91K exec/s over 30s with ASan+LSan. Harness leak fix from bd-s83dv integrated. Verified 2026-04-23."}

- For stat family: non-negative rc implies st_dev/st_ino written; errno on failure is in the documented set
- Host parity: our errno family matches libc for inert paths

## Seed corpus
- empty, '/', '.', '..', '/tmp', '/proc/self', '/dev/null', '/etc/passwd', '/////' (collapsing slashes), '../../../..', path with unicode (é, 中, emoji), path with high-bit bytes, path at NAME_MAX (255), path at PATH_MAX (4096)

Intended as the next bd-h5x raw-syscall slice; this br build rejects --parent=bd-h5x as ambiguous.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T00:26:03.272127408Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T00:30:04.384438727Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T00:30:04.382542038Z","close_reason":"Added typed raw futex_waitv veneer in frankenlibc-core with focused syscall veneer coverage and passed rch core gates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-aynq","title":"syscall: sched_rr_get_interval nonexistent pid with null output should preserve ESRCH","description":"Host glibc returns ESRCH for sched_rr_get_interval(huge_nonexistent_pid, NULL), so the nonexistent-pid contract dominates the null output pointer. Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:49:26.231021059Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T02:04:56.856907863Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T02:04:56.854713987Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -759,7 +759,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-kx74.1","title":"Implement per-thread re-entrancy guard for membrane validation pipeline","description":"Add a thread-local boolean flag MEMBRANE_REENTRY that skips validation when a thread is already inside the pipeline. This prevents the RwLock deadlock that caused signal_runtime_ready to be removed.

APPROACH:
1. Add thread_local! { static MEMBRANE_ACTIVE: Cell<bool> = Cell::new(false); }
2. In runtime_policy::decide(), check MEMBRANE_ACTIVE first — if true, return passthrough
3. Set MEMBRANE_ACTIVE to true before entering the pipeline, false after
4. This allows nested calls (malloc inside validation) to bypass the pipeline

RISK: Some functions SHOULD be validated even during nested calls. The per-thread flag is a coarse solution. A more nuanced approach would be per-API-family re-entrancy tracking.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:55.120006400Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:46:37.715103006Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:46:37.714070612Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-kx74.1","depends_on_id":"bd-kx74","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:55.120006400Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-kx74.2","title":"Re-enable signal_runtime_ready() in startup paths","description":"Once the re-entrancy guard is in place, re-enable signal_runtime_ready() in both:
1. host_delegate_main_wrapper (startup_abi.rs:422)
2. The fallback __libc_start_main path (startup_abi.rs:855)

TEST: Run the full 61-program suite + complex programs with membrane active. Verify no hangs or crashes. Measure overhead.

DEPENDS ON: Re-entrancy guard must be implemented first.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:56.339062891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:46:58.606068078Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:46:58.605102992Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-kx74.2","depends_on_id":"bd-kx74","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:56.339062891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-kx74.2","depends_on_id":"bd-kx74.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:57.559397522Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-kx74.2","depends_on_id":"bd-s39o","type":"related","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:26.058213823Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-l036","title":"unwind: execute backtrace_ops.json fixture family through harness matrix","description":"Concrete gap: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/backtrace_ops_conformance_test.rs currently stops at fixture metadata and coverage counts. Add isolated harness subprocess execution for tests/conformance/fixtures/backtrace_ops.json, assert expected_output and host parity by mode, and fix any matrix-path regressions the new coverage exposes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:52:10.052034129Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:59:13.053475758Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:59:13.051484167Z","close_reason":"Added isolated harness-matrix execution for backtrace_ops.json, modeled the hardened corrupted-stack fixture explicitly, and verified locally and via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-l0ykk","title":"fuzz: env var handling (getenv/setenv/putenv/unsetenv/secure_getenv)","description":"## Scope
Add crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_env.rs targeting frankenlibc_abi::{stdlib_abi,unistd_abi} env-var functions: getenv, setenv, unsetenv, putenv, secure_getenv, clearenv. These mutate process-global state (environ[]) and are security-sensitive (LD_PRELOAD, LD_LIBRARY_PATH, TZ, LANG parsing).

## Archetype
4 stateful + 3 differential:
- Maintain a shadow BTreeMap<Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>> model of 'expected environ state'
- Each iteration picks a random op (set/unset/put/get) and an arbitrary name + value
- After each op, walk environ[] and assert our shadow model matches
- For get, assert ours == libc::getenv on the same name

## Inputs
struct EnvFuzzInput {
  ops: Vec<EnvOp>,            // up to 32 ops per iteration
}

enum EnvOp {
  Get(name: Vec<u8>),
  Set { name: Vec<u8>, value: Vec<u8>, overwrite: bool },
  Unset(name: Vec<u8>),
  Put(entry: Vec<u8>),        // 'NAME=VALUE' combined — tests '=' presence check
  Clear,
}

## Invariants
- All ops return documented error codes (0/-1 with EINVAL/ENOMEM) or valid pointer/NULL for get
- No panic on embedded NUL / missing '=' / empty-name / name-with-'=' edge cases
- After setenv+getenv round-trip: ours matches what was set (modulo CString boundary)
- After unsetenv: getenv returns NULL for that name
- environ[] ordering is deterministic after equivalent op sequences
- secure_getenv returns NULL when AT_SECURE is set (if we can simulate) — or matches getenv otherwise
- LD_PRELOAD / LD_LIBRARY_PATH must NEVER echo back to getenv after setenv when the process is secure (sanity check the membrane policy's AT_SECURE gating)

## Expected bug classes
- setenv with name-containing-'=' accepting instead of EINVAL
- putenv pointing into freed memory (glibc contract: putenv takes ownership)
- environ[] not resized on set/unset
- Race between setenv calls (if we later add per-thread env)

## Score
5 × 5 × /3 ≈ 8.3 — LD_PRELOAD injection is one of the top-3 linux privesc vectors, and any regression in env handling silently breaks thousands of CLI tools. Setenv CVEs appear in every glibc release notes page.","notes":"Closed by cc-libc (JSONL edit — br CLI lookup bug pattern continues).

Delivered crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_env.rs as a stateful fuzzer driving getenv / secure_getenv / setenv / unsetenv / putenv / clearenv against a shadow BTreeMap<Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>> model.

Design:
- All fuzzer-supplied names are rewritten to `FL_FUZZ_<ascii-alnum-tail>` so we never clobber system env (PATH, HOME, LD_*, TZ, LANG).
- Each iteration holds a global ENV_LOCK, calls clearenv() to zero process env, and initializes an empty shadow. Every EnvOp (Get/GetSecure/Set/SetMalformed/Unset/Put/Clear) mutates both the ABI and the shadow, then the op-loop finishes with a full-shadow re-scan that asserts getenv(name) bytes match the shadow for every key.
- SetMalformed deliberately passes name containing '=' or empty string; asserts ABI returns -1 (POSIX EINVAL).
- Put leaks its 'NAME=VALUE\0' buffer into a PUTENV_ARENA so the pointer putenv holds never dangles for the process lifetime (glibc contract).
- Bounds: ≤32 ops/iteration, name ≤32 bytes, value ≤128 bytes.

Differential against libc::getenv isn't done for the same reason as bd-anoe7: both the ABI layer and libc export `getenv`, so a naive extern "C" binds only one impl. The shadow model is the oracle and catches every contract regression locally without needing two independent getenv implementations.

Wired as `fuzz_env` bin in crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml.

Validation: `cargo build --bin fuzz_env` clean (0.59s incremental after unused-import fix); `cargo clippy --bin fuzz_env --no-deps -- -D warnings` clean.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cc-libc","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:40:38.338214888Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:31:04.930846Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:31:04.930846Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l0ykk","depends_on_id":"bd-jy4qu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:01:47.549218159Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Work landed in commit 9f24fcd4 (feat(fuzz): add fuzz_env shadow-model stateful harness).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l0ykk","depends_on_id":"bd-jy4qu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:01:47.549218159Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-l2r","title":"Kernel: Alpha-Investing FDR (tests + perf evidence)","description":"Prove correctness and measure overhead for alpha-investing integration.

Tests:
- Simulated null stream: verify empirical false discovery is controlled (within expected bounds).
- Optional stopping simulation: ensure controller remains conservative.
- Regression tests for deterministic outcomes.

Perf:
- Microbench decide/observe deltas in strict/hardened.
- Confirm no exp/ln/matrix solve appears on strict hot path.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleOwl","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:32:08.403251183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:34:51.136876068Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:34:51.136858715Z","close_reason":"All 19 alpha_investing tests pass: FDR bounded, integer-only hot path verified, kernel integration confirmed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l2r","depends_on_id":"bd-242","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l2r","depends_on_id":"bd-3kz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l2r","depends_on_id":"bd-d5l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-l2yj","title":"pthread_sigqueue_preserves_siginfo_and_ucontext_after_deferred_replay fails with ESRCH","description":"Test fails consistently with 'pthread_sigqueue must succeed while the worker is critical: left=3 right=0'. Error 3 is ESRCH (No such process), returned by rt_sigqueueinfo when the target TID is stale. The test poisons TEST_GUARD mutex, cascading PoisonError into 14 other tests. Root cause likely: (a) worker thread ID captured before worker enters critical section becomes stale if worker exits before pthread_sigqueue dispatch, or (b) pthread_sigqueue TID lookup races with thread registration.
